prompt: Write a JavaScript function `slugify(s)` that: lowercases the input, trims it, replaces every run of non-alphanumeric characters with a single hyphen, and removes leading/trailing hyphens. Output ONLY the function definition — no exports, no imports, no markdown fences, no explanation.

grader.assertions: if (slugify('  Hello, World!  ') !== 'hello-world') throw new Error('basic: '+slugify('  Hello, World!  ')); if (slugify('a__b--c') !== 'a-b-c') throw new Error('runs'); if (slugify('--Trim--') !== 'trim') throw new Error('trim-edges'); if (slugify('Already-Clean') !== 'already-clean') throw new Error('clean');

notes: Real codegen graded by EXECUTING the output against hidden assertions (pass/fail is objective, not vibes). The closest local analogue to the builder agent's actual job. SWE-bench in miniature.
